title: ai-squad Session
description: Canonical contract for the runtime Session state at .agent-session/<spec_id>/session.yml. Single source of truth for the most-consumed runtime artifact (cockpit observers derive status, tasks, timeline and escalation metrics from it). Hand-authored as plain YAML by the Phase Skills (atomic tmp+rename, no PyYAML) and by register-impl-session.py (pure text edit). This schema documents every real field and the discriminated shape of notes; it is NOT enforced at write time. Consumers must validate on read and treat unknown shapes as drift.

spec_id.description: Session/feature id; must match the .agent-session/<spec_id>/ directory name. Legacy sessions used task_id for this field; readers accept task_id as an alias.

task_id.description: Deprecated alias of spec_id, present only on legacy Sessions. New Sessions write spec_id.

feature_name.description: Human-readable Spec title; populated by spec-writer at first draft.

schema_version.description: Schema version anchor for future migration. Currently 1 (first machine schema).

squad.description: Which squad owns the Session. Absent defaults to sdd.

plan_ref.description: Path to plan.md; may be absent if Phase 2 not planned.

tasks_ref.description: Path to tasks.md; may be absent if Phase 3 not planned.

completed_at.description: ISO 8601 or empty string; set on done/escalated/paused.

current_phase.description: Current Session phase. 'paused' is terminal-but-resumable. Consumers detect paused via this field — there is no separate paused_at field.

current_owner.description: Role with write authority for the current phase.

output_locale.description: BCP-47 tag; language of human-facing prose. Absent defaults to en.

pipeline_mode.description: Pipeline mode chosen at spec-writer entry.

auto_approved_by.description: Set to 'pm' by /pm at autonomous-run entry; absent on interactive runs.

pm_cost_cap_usd.description: Optional PM cost cap; null/absent means no cap.

planned_phases.description: Phases selected to run, in order.

implementation_sessions.description: Claude Code session ids that dispatched Phase 4 work; appended by register-impl-session.py. Authoritative cost-scoping allow-list.

pipeline_started_at.description: Phase 4 start; empty until pipeline starts.

task_states.description: Per-task state for Phase 4, keyed by task id (T-XXX).

packet_retries.description: Re-dispatches due to missing/invalid Output Packet (cap packet_retry_max=2).

budget_defaults.description: Loop budget caps for Phase 4, inherited by task_states unless overridden per dispatch.

review_loops_max.description: Max review loops per task before escalation.

qa_loops_max.description: Max QA loops per task before escalation.

blocker_calls_max.description: Max blocker-specialist calls per task before escalation.

escalation_metrics.description: Aggregate escalation health for this run.

pending_human_tasks.description: Tasks parked in pending_human (escalated to the human).

escalation_rate.description: pending_human_tasks / total_tasks, as a fraction in [0,1] (healthy ~0.10-0.15).

phase_history.description: One entry per phase, appended as phases complete or are skipped.

completed_at.description: ISO 8601; empty if running or skipped.

artifact_status.description: Status of the phase artifact, e.g. approved.

skipped.description: True when the phase was not in planned_phases.

approved_by.description: Who approved the artifact (e.g. pm for autonomous runs).

pipeline_summary.description: Phase 4 only: short summary of the pipeline outcome.

notes.description: Append-only governance/audit event log. Always a list of typed objects discriminated by kind — never a scalar. Consumed by audit-agent (pm_decision reconciliation, AC-017) and by external observers (timeline).
